intro: Kaulinia pteropus, also known as Pterocarpus marsupium, is a deciduous tree species native to India and Southeast Asia, commonly referred to as the "Indian Red Sandalwood." The health benefits of Kaulinia pteropus include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include pterocarpin, pterocarpidol, and other flavonoids and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Kaulinia pteropus is commonly used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine and is often prepared as a decoction, powder, or oil, with the latter being used topically for skin conditions and the former being consumed as a treatment for various health issues.
